research.title: Research
currentProjects: Causal inference for complex systems: Novel tools for understanding causal interactions particularly in high dimensional systems/ networks. Applications to climate, paleoclimate and neuroscience.

EEG analysis for prediction of early response to antidepressants: EEG based brain connectivity and complexity analysis for patients suffering from Major Depressive Disorder. Developing methods for identifying patients who will respond to antidepressant treatment in a timely manner.

Hybrid neurochaos Learning-reservoir computing architecture: Novel machine learning architecture using neurochaos learning in reservoir computing framework for prediction of time series from dynamical systems.
pastProjects: PhD Thesis: Theoretical and Experimental Investigations into Causality, its Measures and Applications. Thesis Advisor: Dr. Nithin Nagaraj, NIAS. Determining cause-effect relationships between variables based on time-series measurements has widespread applications in several scientific disciplines. Model-free measures of causality estimation that do not impose an underlying causal mechanism on the data are especially useful in this regard. This research aimed at developing novel data-based causality measures that make less assumptions and can make causal inferences by interventional means. The methods were tested on simulated and real-world data. In another line of work, causality conditions were provided for synchronization in the case of coupled dynamical systems.

Masters Thesis: A Robust Ultrasound Doppler Spectral Envelope Detection Technique for Automated Blood Flow Measurements. Thesis Advisors: Prof. Hans Torp and Prof. Sturla Eik Nes, Norwegian University of Science and Technology (NTNU), Norway. Accurate maximum velocities and spectral envelope estimation based on ultrasound Doppler blood flow spectrograms are essential for clinical diagnostic purposes. However, due to intrinsic spectral broadening in the power spectrum it becomes difficult to accurately determine these. In this work, we proposed a novel signal processing based method for maximum velocity detection. Data adaptive elimination of erroneous velocity estimates helped yield a smoother spectral envelope for clinical indices estimation.

affiliations.title: Previous Affiliations
items: 2021-2024: Postdoctoral Researcher, Institute of Computer Science of the Czech Academy of Sciences, Czech Republic. Projects: Interactions in Complex systems: Causal discovery from irregularly sampled data and applications on climate data, Causal discovery from sparse data; Brain connectivity analysis for depression and its treatment.

2015-2016: Research Assistant, Norwegian University of Science and Technology (NTNU), Norway. Projects: Ultrasound Doppler Signal Processing and Spectral Envelope Detection, Experimental studies for low-cost finger Doppler device for early diabetic assessment, Safety measurements for different transducer settings to obtain ethical approval for patient studies.
2014 Summer: Research Intern, GE Global Research Centre, JFWTC, Bengaluru, India. Project: Quantitative Ultrasound for Characterization of Liver Phantoms.

2013 Summer: Research Intern, Maternal Health Technology Division, Vivekananda Tribal Hospital, Bastar, India. Project: To Explore the Scope of a Low End Doppler Machine in Determining Feto-Maternal Health.
